// add+modify_eas_records.hpp
#pragma once

#include <filesystem>
#include <map>
#include <optional>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sstream>
#include <stdexcept>
#include <string>
#include <string_view>
#include <type_traits>
#include <utility>
#include <vector>

#include "eas_common.hpp"
using namespace sqlgen;
using namespace sqlgen::literals;

namespace {

template <class T>
struct TableWrapper {};

consteval std::optional<std::string> get_primary_key_column(
    std::string_view _table_name) noexcept {
  static constexpr std::map<std::string, std::string> primary_keys = {
      {"Student", "SNo"},
      {"Course", "CID"},
      {"Score", "ScID"},
      {"Major", "MID"}};
  const auto& it = primary_keys.find(_table_name);
  if (it == primary_keys.end()) {
    return std::nullopt;
  }
  return it->second;
}

template <class T>
struct TypeHolder {};

template <class T>
consteval std::type_index get_primary_key_type() noexcept {
  constexpr std::string_view primary_key = get_primary_key_column<T>();
  static constexpr std::map<std::string, std::type_index> primary_types = {
      {"SNo", typeid(std::string)},
      {"CID", typeid(uint32_t)},
      {"ScID", typeid(uint32_t)},
      {"MID", typeid(uint32_t)}};
  return primary_types.at(primary_key.str());
}

template <class T, class Value>
std::optional<Value> convert_primary_key(
    const std::optional<std::string>& _str) noexcept {
  using PKType = std::remove_cvref_t<typename TypeHolder<T>::PKType>;
  if constexpr (std::is_same_v<PKType, std::string>) {
    return _str;
  } else if constexpr (std::is_integral_v<PKType> ||
                std::is_floating_point_v<PKType>) {
    try {
      if (_str) {
        return std::stoi(_str->str());
      } else {
        return std::nullopt;
      }
    } catch (const std::exception&) {
      return std::nullopt;
    }
  } else {
    return std::nullopt;
  }
}

}  // namespace

void add_eas_records(const std::string& _table_name,
                     const std::vector<std::tuple<std::string, std::string, int, std::optional<sqlgen::DateTime>, std::optional<uint32_t>, std::optional<double>, std::optional<std::string>>>& _records) {
  const auto conn = sqlite::connect(db_path).value();

  if (_table_name == "Student") {
    for (const auto& _record : _records) {
      Student student;
      student.SNo = std::get<0>(_record);
      student.SName = std::get<1>(_record);
      student.Sex = std::get<2>(_record);
      student.Birthday = std::get<3>(_record);
      student.Major = std::get<4>(_record);
      student.Scorses = std::get<5>(_record);
      student.Remark = std::get<6>(_record);
      conn.insert(student);
    }
  } else if (_table_name == "Course") {
    for (const auto& _record : _records) {
      Course course;
      course.CID = std::get<0>(_record);
      course.CName = std::get<1>(_record);
      course.Term = std::get<2>(_record);
      course.CTime = std::get<3>(_record);
      course.Credit = std::get<4>(_record);
      conn.insert(course);
    }
  } else if (_table_name == "Score") {
    for (const auto& _record : _records) {
      Score score;
      score.ScID = std::get<0>(_record);
      score.SNo = std::get<1>(_record);
      score.CID = std::get<2>(_record);
      score.Score = std::get<3>(_record);
      conn.insert(score);
    }
  } else if (_table_name == "Major") {
    for (const auto& _record : _records) {
      Major major;
      major.MID = std::get<0>(_record);
      major.MName = std::get<1>(_record);
      conn.insert(major);
    }
  } else {
    throw std::runtime_error("Unknown table name: " + _table_name);
  }
}

void modify_eas_records_by_id(const std::string& _table_name,
                              const std::string& _record_id,
                              const std::string& _col_name,
                              const std::string& _new_value) {
  const auto conn = sqlite::connect(db_path).value();

  const auto primary_key_col = get_primary_key_column(_table_name);
  if (!primary_key_col) {
    throw std::runtime_error("Unknown table name: " + _table_name);
  }

  const auto primary_key_type = get_primary_key_type<_table_name>();
  const auto parsed_id = convert_primary_key<_table_name>(_record_id);

  if (!parsed_id) {
    throw std::runtime_error("Invalid record ID: " + _record_id);
  }

  const std::string query =
      "UPDATE " + _table_name + " SET " + _col_name + " = '" + _new_value +
      "' WHERE " + *primary_key_col + " = " +
      std::to_string(*parsed_id) + ";";

  const auto res = conn.execute(_table_name, query);
  if (!res) {
    throw std::runtime_error("Failed to execute query: " + res.error().what());
  }
}

void delete_eas_records_by_id(const std::string& _table_name,
                              const std::string& _record_id) {
  const auto conn = sqlite::connect(db_path).value();

  const auto primary_key_col = get_primary_key_column(_table_name);
  if (!primary_key_col) {
    throw std::runtime_error("Unknown table name: " + _table_name);
  }

  const auto primary_key_type = get_primary_key_type<_table_name>();
  const auto parsed_id = convert_primary_key<_table_name>(_record_id);

  if (!parsed_id) {
    throw std::runtime_error("Invalid record ID: " + _record_id);
  }

  const std::string query = "DELETE FROM " + _table_name + " WHERE " +
                              *primary_key_col + " = " +
                              std::to_string(*parsed_id) + ";";

  const auto res = conn.execute(_table_name, query);
  if (!res) {
    throw std::runtime_error("Failed to execute query: " + res.error().what());
  }
}

<answer>```cpp
#include <filesystem>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

namespace test_create_eas_database {
  void create_eas_database() {
    const auto dir = "data";
    if (!std::filesystem::exists(dir)) {
      std::filesystem::create_directory(dir);
    }

    const auto main_db_path = "test_db.sqlite";
    const auto eas_db_path = "data/eas_database.db";

    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection::make(main_db_path);
    if (!conn) {
      // Handle connection error if necessary
      return;
    }

    const auto attach_sql = "ATTACH DATABASE '" + eas_db_path + "' AS EAS_DB;";
    conn->execute(attach_sql);
  }
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <stdexcept>
#include <string>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

#include "delete_inactive_users.hpp"

namespace delete_inactive_users {

struct User {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string username;
  std::optional<sqlgen::DateTime> last_login;
  std::string status = "active";
};

int delete_inactive_users() {
  try {
    const auto conn = sqlgen::make_connection<sqlgen::SQLite>("test_db.sqlite");
    const auto begin_tx = conn->begin_transaction();
    if (!begin_tx) {
      return begin_tx.error();
    }

    const auto cutoff_date = sqlgen::DateTime{2025, 1, 1};
    const auto query =
        sqlgen::delete_from<User>().where(
            [&](const auto& _user) { return _user.last_login && *_user.last_login < cutoff_date; }));

    const auto res = conn->execute(query);
    if (!res) {
      const auto rollback = conn->rollback();
      return rollback ? res.error() : rollback.error();
    }

    return conn->commit() ? 0 : conn->error().value();
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    return -1;
  }
}
}  // namespace delete_inactive_users

Then execute the query.

This approach is better because it avoids hardcoding the date strings and uses proper DateTime objects, which sqlgen can handle correctly.

So putting all together, the code would be:

#include <optional>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>
#include <chrono>
#include <iomanip>
#include <sstream>

namespace query_active_users_posts {

struct User {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string username;
};

struct Post {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> post_id;
  int user_id;
  sqlgen::DateTime created_at;
};

struct ActiveUser {
  static constexpr const char* tablename = "ActiveUserView";
  static constexpr bool is_view = true;
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string username;
};

std::vector<ActiveUser> query_active_users_posts() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  
  // Create tables
  conn.create_table<User>();
  conn.create_table<Post>();
  
  // Define date range
  const auto start_date = sqlgen::DateTime{2025, 12, 1};
  const auto end_date = sqlgen::DateTime{2025, 12, 31};
  
  // Build the query
  const auto query = sqlgen::from<User>("u")
      .inner_join<Post>("p", "u.user_id = p.user_id")
      .where("p.created_at >= ? AND p.created_at <= ?", start_date, end_date)
      .select("u.user_id", "u.username")
      .distinct();
  
  // Execute the query and map results
  return conn.execute(query)
      .transform([](auto&& it) {
        std::vector<ActiveUser> result;
        for (auto& row : it) {
          ActiveUser au;
          au.user_id = row.get<int>("user_id");
          au.username = row.get<std::string>("username");
          result.emplace_back(std::move(au));
        }
        return result;
      })
      .value_or(std::vector<ActiveUser>());
}

}  // namespace query_active_users_posts

Now, code:

#include <optional>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sstream>
#include <stdexcept>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

#include "query_course_enrollment_stats.hpp"

namespace query_course_enrollment_stats {

std::vector<CourseStats> query_course_enrollment_stats() {
  try {
    // Connect to the SQLite database.
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection::make("test_db.sqlite");

    if (!conn) {
      throw std::runtime_error("Failed to connect to database.");
    }

    // Create Course table.
    const std::string create_course_table =
        "CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS Course ("
        "course_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, "
        "title TEXT NOT NULL);";
    conn->execute(create_course_table);

    // Create Enrollment table.
    const std::string create_enrollment_table =
        "CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS Enrollment ("
        "enrollment_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, "
        "course_id INTEGER, "
        "grade REAL, "
        "FOREIGN KEY(course_id) REFERENCES Course(course_id));";
    conn->execute(create_enrollment_table);

    // Execute the query to get enrollment statistics.
    const std::string query =
        "SELECT c.title, COUNT(e.enrollment_id) AS student_count, "
        "AVG(e.grade) AS avg_grade "
        "FROM Course c "
        "LEFT JOIN Enrollment e ON c.course_id = e.course_id "
        "GROUP BY c.course_id, c.title;";
    const auto result = conn->execute(query);

    if (!result) {
      throw std::runtime_error("Query execution failed: " +
                               std::string(result.error().what()));
    }

    // Read the results into CourseStats objects.
    std::vector<CourseStats> stats;
    for (const auto& row : result->read_all()) {
      CourseStats cs;
      cs.title = row["title"].as<std::string>();
      cs.student_count = row["student_count"].as<int>();
      cs.avg_grade = row["avg_grade"].transform(
          [](const auto& val) { return val.as<double>(); });
      stats.push_back(std::move(cs));
    }

    return stats;
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    // Handle exceptions (e.g., log the error).
    std::cerr << "Error: " << e.what() << std::endl;
    return {};
  }
}

}  // namespace query_course_enrollment_stats

#endif

#include <ranges>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>

#include <vector>

namespace query_customer_purchase_history {

struct Customer {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> customer_id;
  std::string name;
  std::string email;
};

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  std::string name;
  double price;
};

struct Order {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> order_id;
  sqlgen::ForeignKey<int, Customer, "customer_id"> customer_id;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> order_date;
  double total_amount;
};

struct OrderItem {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> item_id;
  sqlgen::ForeignKey<int, Order, "order_id"> order_id;
  sqlgen::ForeignKey<int, Product, "product_id"> product_id;
  int quantity;
};

struct PurchaseHistory {
  std::string product_name;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> order_date;
  int quantity;
};

std::vector<PurchaseHistory> query_customer_purchase_history(int customer_id) {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection::make("test_db.sqlite").value();

  // Create tables
  conn.write_schema<Customer>();
  conn.write_schema<Order>();
  conn.write_schema<OrderItem>();
  conn.write_schema<Product>();

  // Execute the query
  const auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Customer, Order, OrderItem, Product>(
      conn)
      .where("Customer.customer_id = ?")
      .fields("Product.name", "Order.order_date", "OrderItem.quantity")
      .order_by("Order.order_date DESC")
      .transform([](auto range) {
        return sqlgen::collect/vector<PurchaseHistory>(range);
      });

  return query.value_or(std::vector<PurchaseHistory>());
}

}  // namespace query_customer_purchase_history

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <ranges>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

#include "query_daily_traffic_metrics.hpp"

namespace query_daily_traffic_metrics {

std::vector<DailyTraffic> query_daily_traffic_metrics() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

  const auto query = sqlgen::select_from<PageVisit>(
      sqlgen::as(sqlgen::strftime("%Y-%m-%d", sqlgen::col("visit_time")), "visit_date"),
      sqlgen::count().as("total_visits"),
      sqlgen::count(sqlgen::distinct(sqlgen::col("url"))).as("unique_pages")
  )
      .group_by("visit_date")
      .order_by("visit_date");

  const auto res = conn->execute(query);

  std::vector<DailyTraffic> result;
  for (const auto& row : res) {
    result.emplace_back(
        std::string(rfl::get<0>(row)),
        rfl::get<1>(row),
        rfl::get<2>(row));
  }

  return result;
}

}  // namespace query_daily_traffic_metrics
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <optional>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

#include "query_employee_hierarchy.hpp"

namespace query_employee_hierarchy {

struct Employee {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> emp_id;
  std::string name;
  std::optional<int> manager_id;
};

struct EmployeeManager {
  std::string employee;
  std::optional<std::string> manager;
};

std::vector<EmployeeManager> query_employee_hierarchy() {
  try {
    const auto conn = sqlgen::make_connection<sqlite::Connection>("test_db.sqlite");
    const auto query = conn->from<Employee>().alias("e1")
        .left_join<Employee>().alias("e2").on("e1.manager_id = e2.emp_id")
        .select("e1.name", "e2.name")
        .to<std::vector<std::vector<std::optional<std::string>>>>();

    std::vector<EmployeeManager> result;
    for (const auto& row : query) {
      result.emplace_back(
          .employee = row[0],
          .manager = row[1]);
    }
    return result;
  } catch (std::exception& e) {
    // Handle exceptions as needed, but rethrow to propagate
    throw;
  }
}

}  // namespace query_employee_hierarchy
```</answer>

class OrderView:

#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>

#include <vector>

namespace query_search_books_by_criteria {

struct Book {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> book_id;
  std::string title;
  std::string author;
  int year;
  std::string genre;
  double price;
};

struct BookFilters {
  std::optional<std::string> title_keyword;
  std::optional<int> min_year;
  std::optional<std::string> genre;
  std::optional<double> max_price;
};

std::vector<Book> query_search_books_by_criteria(const BookFilters& filters) {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;

  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

  auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Book>();

  auto condition = Nothing;

  const auto& book = query.alias();

  if (filters.title_keyword) {
    auto title_condition = book.title.like("%" + *filters.title_keyword + "%");
    condition = title_condition;
  }

  if (filters.min_year) {
    auto year_condition = book.year >= *filters.min_year;
    condition = condition && year_condition;
  }

  if (filters.genre) {
    auto genre_condition = book.genre == *filters.genre;
    condition = condition && genre_condition;
  }

  if (filters.max_price) {
    auto price_condition = book.price <= *filters.max_price;
    condition = condition && price_condition;
  }

  if (condition) {
    query.where(condition);
  }

  auto it_begin = query.begin(*conn);
  auto it_end = query.end();

  return std::vector<Book>(it_begin, it_end);
}

}  // namespace query_search_books_by_criteria

#endif

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

namespace schema_migration_add_column {

struct User {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string username;
};

struct UserAfter {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int, sqlgen::auto_incr> user_id;
  std::string username;
  std::string phone_number;
  bool is_active;
};

bool migration_add_column() {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

    // Check if 'phone_number' column exists
    auto check_phone = conn.execute("PRAGMA table_info(User);");
    bool phone_exists = false;
    for (const auto& row : check_phone) {
      if (row["name"].as<std::string>() == "phone_number") {
        phone_exists = true;
        break;
      }
    }
    if (!phone_exists) {
      conn.execute("ALTER TABLE User ADD COLUMN phone_number VARCHAR(20);");
    }

    // Check if 'is_active' column exists
    auto check_active = conn.execute("PRAGMA table_info(User);");
    bool active_exists = false;
    for (const auto& row : check_active) {
      if (row["name"].as<std::string>() == "is_active") {
        active_exists = true;
        break;
      }
    }
    if (!active_exists) {
      conn.execute("ALTER TABLE User ADD COLUMN is_active INTEGER DEFAULT 1;");
    }

    return true;
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    return false;
  }
}

} // namespace schema_migration_add_column
</answer>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>

namespace soft_delete_records {

struct Document {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> doc_id;
  std::string title;
  int is_deleted = 0;
  std::optional<sqlgen::DateTime> deleted_at;
};

bool soft_delete_records(const std::vector<int>& ids) {
  try {
    if (ids.empty()) {
      return true;
    }

    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection("test_db.sqlite");
    auto session = sqlgen::Session(conn);

    session.begin_transaction();

    auto query = session.prepare_update<Document>("Document",
                                                 {{"is_deleted", 1},
                                                  {"deleted_at", "CURRENT_TIMESTAMP"}},
                                                 where(Document::doc_id.is_in(ids)));

    query.execute();

    session.commit();
    return true;
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    return false;
  }
}
}  // namespace soft_delete_records

<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

namespace transaction_transfer_funds {

struct Account {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> account_id;
  double balance;
};

bool transfer_funds(int from_account_id, int to_account_id, double amount) {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
    conn->begin_transaction();

    // Deduct from sender, ensuring sufficient balance
    auto deduct_stmt = conn->prepare(
        "UPDATE Account SET balance = balance - ? WHERE account_id = ? AND balance >= ?");
    deduct_stmt.bind(1, amount);
    deduct_stmt.bind(2, from_account_id);
    deduct_stmt.bind(3, amount);
    const auto deduct_res = deduct_stmt.execute();
    if (deduct_res.rows_affected != 1) {
      conn->rollback();
      return false;
    }

    // Add to receiver
    auto add_stmt = conn->prepare(
        "UPDATE Account SET balance = balance + ? WHERE account_id = ?");
    add_stmt.bind(1, amount);
    add_stmt.bind(2, to_account_id);
    const auto add_res = add_stmt.execute();
    if (add_res.rows_affected != 1) {
      conn->rollback();
      return false;
    }

    conn->commit();
    return true;
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    // Handle connection errors or other exceptions
    return false;
  }
}
}  // namespace transaction_transfer_funds
```</answer>

// eas_common.hpp
#pragma once

#include <filesystem>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

using namespace sqlgen::literals;

struct Student {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<sqlgen::Varchar<10>> SNo;
  sqlgen::Varchar<30> SName;
  int Sex = 1;
  std::optional<sqlgen::DateTime> Birthday;
  std::optional<uint32_t> Major;
  std::optional<double> Scores = 0.0;
  std::optional<std::string> Remark;
};

struct Course {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<uint32_t, sqlgen::auto_incr> CID;
  sqlgen::Varchar<50> CName;
  std::optional<int> Term = 1;
  std::optional<int> CTime;
  int Credit = 0;
};

struct Score {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<uint32_t, sqlgen::auto_incr> ScID;
  sqlgen::ForeignKey<sqlgen::Varchar<10>, Student, "SNo"> SNo;
  sqlgen::ForeignKey<uint32_t, Course, "CID"> CID;
  std::optional<double> Score;
};

struct Major {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<uint32_t> MID;
  sqlgen::Varchar<30> MName;
};

const std::string db_path = "data/eas_database.db";

void design_eas_database_without_major() {
  std::filesystem::create_directories("data");
  sqlgen::sqlite::connect(db_path)
      .and_then([](auto&& conn) {
        return sqlgen::exec(conn, "PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;");
      })
      .and_then(sqlgen::create_table<Student> | sqlgen::if_not_exists)
      .and_then(sqlgen::create_table<Course> | sqlgen::if_not_exists)
      .and_then(sqlgen::create_table<Score> | sqlgen::if_not_exists)
      .value();
}

void design_eas_database_with_major() {
  design_eas_database_without_major();

  sqlgen::sqlite::connect(db_path)
      .and_then(sqlgen::create_table<Major> | sqlgen::if_not_exists)
      .value();
}

#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>

#include "eas_common.hpp"

namespace update_eas_table {

void update_eas_table() {
  sqlgen::sqlite::connect(db_path)
      .and_then([](auto& conn) {
        // Add Grade column to Score table
        return conn.execute("ALTER TABLE Score ADD COLUMN Grade CHAR(1) NOT NULL;");
      })
      .and_then([](const auto&) {
        // Modify Age column in Student table
        return sqlgen::sqlite::connect(db_path)
            .and_then([](auto& conn) { return conn.execute("ALTER TABLE Student MODIFY COLUMN Age INT NULL;"); });
      })
      .and_then([](const auto&) {
        // Drop Major table
        return sqlgen::sqlite::connect(db_path)
            .and_then([](auto& conn) { return conn.execute("DROP TABLE Major;"); });
      })
      .value();
}

} // namespace update_eas_table
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <ranges>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

#include "schema_create_view_sales_summary.hpp"

namespace schema_create_view_sales_summary {

bool create_view_sales_summary() {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::make_connection<sqlgen::SQLite>("test_db.sqlite");

    // Check if the view already exists
    const auto check_query = "SELECT 1 FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='view' AND name='SalesSummary';";
    const auto check_result = conn->execute(check_query);

    if (!check_result) {
      return false;
    }

    bool view_exists = false;
    for (const auto& row : *check_result) {
      view_exists = true;
      break;
    }

    if (view_exists) {
      // Drop the existing view
      const auto drop_query = "DROP VIEW SalesSummary;";
      const auto drop_result = conn->execute(drop_query);
      if (!drop_result) {
        return false;
      }
    }

    // Create the new view
    const auto create_view_query = R"(
      CREATE VIEW SalesSummary AS
      SELECT p.name as product_name, SUM(s.quantity) as total_quantity, SUM(s.amount) as total_revenue
      FROM Sale s
      JOIN Product p ON s.product_id = p.product_id
      GROUP BY p.name;
    )";

    const auto create_result = conn->execute(create_view_query);
    return create_result ? true : false;
  } catch (const std::exception& e) {
    return false;
  }
}

}  // namespace schema_create_view_sales_summary
```</answer>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace generate_sql_dry_run {

struct A {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  std::string name;
};

struct B {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  int a_id;
  double score;
};

std::string generate_sql_dry_run() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection::make("test_db.sqlite").value();
  auto session = conn->session();

  const auto query = session->selectFrom<A>()
      .fields(A_.name, B_.score)
      .join<B>(sqlgen::JoinType::inner_join, "a.id = b.a_id")
      .where(B_.score > 10 && A_.name.like("%x%"))
      .order_by(B_.score.desc())
      .limit(5)
      .to_sql();

  return query;
}
}  // namespace generate_sql_dry_run
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>
#include <optional>
#include <string>

namespace analyze_temporal_data {

struct Event {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> event_id;
  std::string event_name;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> start_time;
  std::optional<sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S">> end_time;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> created_at;
};

struct TemporalAnalysis {
  int event_id;
  std::string event_name;
  int start_day;
  int start_month;
  int start_year;
  int start_hour;
  int start_minute;
  int start_second;
  int start_weekday;
  int64_t start_epoch;
  std::optional<double> duration_days;
};

std::vector<TemporalAnalysis> analyze_temporal_data(const std::vector<Event>& events) {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  conn->insert(events);

  const std::string sql = R"(
    SELECT 
      event_id,
      event_name,
      strftime('%d', start_time) AS start_day,
      strftime('%m', start_time) AS start_month,
      strftime('%Y', start_time) AS start_year,
      strftime('%H', start_time) AS start_hour,
      strftime('%M', start_time) AS start_minute,
      strftime('%S', start_time) AS start_second,
      strftime('%w', start_time) AS start_weekday,
      strftime('%s', start_time) AS start_epoch,
      julianday(end_time) - julianday(start_time) AS duration_days
    FROM Event
    WHERE end_time IS NOT NULL;
  )";

  return conn->execute(sql)
      .transform([](auto&& cursor) -> std::vector<TemporalAnalysis> {
        std::vector<TemporalAnalysis> analyses;
        while (cursor.next()) {
          TemporalAnalysis ta;
          ta.event_id = cursor.get<int>("event_id");
          ta.event_name = cursor.get<std::string>("event_name");
          ta.start_day = cursor.get<int>("start_day");
          ta.start_month = cursor.get<int>("start_month");
          ta.start_year = cursor.get<int>("start_year");
          ta.start_hour = cursor.get<int>("start_hour");
          ta.start_minute = cursor.get<int>("start_minute");
          ta.start_second = cursor.get<int>("start_second");
          ta.start_weekday = cursor.get<int>("start_weekday");
          ta.start_epoch = cursor.get<int64_t>("start_epoch");
          ta.duration_days = cursor.get<std::optional<double>>("duration_days");
          analyses.push_back(std::move(ta));
        }
        return analyses;
      })
      .value_or(std::vector<TemporalAnalysis>());
}
}  // namespace analyze_temporal_data

#include <sqlgen.hpp>

namespace upsert_inventory_items {

struct Inventory {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> item_id;

bool upsert_inventory_items(const std::vector<Inventory>& items) {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
    using InventoryTable = sqlgen::Table<Inventory>;
    conn->upsert<InventoryTable>(items.begin(), items.end(), &Inventory::item_id);

    auto query = sqlgen::select_from<InventoryTable>();

    std::unordered_map<int, Inventory> db_map;
    for (const auto& row : result) {
      db_map[row.item_id] = row;
    }

    for (const auto& item : items) {
      auto it = db_map.find(item.item_id);
      if (it == db_map.end()) return false;
      if (it->second.name != item.name || it->second.quantity != item.quantity) return false;
    }

    return true;
  } catch (...) {
    return false;
  }
}

}  // namespace upsert_inventory_items

For the first query (mutual friends via self-join):

SELECT u1.username, u2.username, COUNT(f1.user_id2) as mutual_friends
FROM User u1
INNER JOIN Friendship f1 ON u1.user_id = f1.user_id1
INNER JOIN Friendship f2 ON f1.user_id2 = f2.user_id1
INNER JOIN User u2 ON f2.user_id2 = u2.user_id
WHERE u1.user_id < u2.user_id
  AND EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM Friendship f3 
    WHERE f3.user_id1 = u1.user_id AND f3.user_id2 = u2.user_id
  )
GROUP BY u1.user_id, u2.user_id
HAVING COUNT(f1.user_id2) > 0;

This query finds pairs of users (u1, u2) who are direct friends (EXISTS clause) and have mutual friends (the count of f1.user_id2, which are friends of u1 that are also friends of u2). The result is pairs of usernames with mutual friends count.

For the friend-of-friend query, we need to find users connected through exactly one mutual friend. So, for example, user A and user C are connected through user B (A is friends with B, B is friends with C, and A and C are not direct friends). So the query would involve joining Friendship twice: once for A-B and once for B-C, and ensuring that A and C are not direct friends.

The SQL for this could be:

SELECT u1.username AS user1, u3.username AS user2
FROM Friendship f1
JOIN Friendship f2 ON f1.user_id2 = f2.user_id1
JOIN User u1 ON f1.user_id1 = u1.user_id
JOIN User u3 ON f2.user_id2 = u3.user_id
WHERE NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM Friendship f3 
    WHERE (f3.user_id1 = f1.user_id1 AND f3.user_id2 = f2.user_id2)
       OR (f3.user_id1 = f2.user_id2 AND f3.user_id1 = f1.user_id1)
)
GROUP BY u1.user_id, u3.user_id
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT f1.user_id2) = 1;

This would find pairs where there's exactly one mutual friend (B) connecting A and C, and they are not direct friends.

For the fifth query (recommendations), find users in the same city not yet friends:

SELECT u1.username, u2.username
FROM User u1
JOIN User u2 ON u1.city = u2.city AND u1.user_id < u2.user_id
WHERE NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM Friendship f WHERE (f.user_id1 = u1.user_id AND f.user_id2 = u2.user_id)
       OR (f.user_id1 = u2.user_id AND f.user_id2 = u1.user_id)
);

This ensures that u1 and u2 are in the same city, not the same user, and not already friends.

def analyze_social_network():
    # Connect to the database
    conn = sqlgen.connect("sqlite:///test_db.sqlite")
    
    # Create tables
    User = conn.make_table(
        "User",
        columns=[
            sqlgen.Column("user_id", sqlgen.types.Int, primary=True),
            sqlgen.Column("username", sqlgen.types.VarChar(50), nullable=False, unique=True),
            sqlgen.Column("email", sqlgen.types.VarChar(100), nullable=False, unique=True),
            sqlgen.Column("join_date", sqlgen.types.DateTime),
            sqlgen.Column("city", sqlgen.types.VarChar(50))
        ]
    )
    Friendship = conn.make_table(
        "Friendship",
        columns=[
            sqlgen.Column("user_id1", sqlgen.types.Int, foreign_key_reference=(User, "user_id"), primary=True),
            sqlgen.Column("user_id2", sqlgen.types.Int, foreign_key_reference=(User, "user_id"), primary=True),
            sqlgen.Column("friendship_date", sqlgen.types.DateTime)
        ],
        primary_keys=["user_id1", "user_id2"]
    )
    
    # Query 1: Mutual friends
    mutual_query = """
    SELECT u1.username, u2.username, COUNT(f1.user_id2) as mutual_friends
    FROM User u1
    INNER JOIN Friendship f1 ON u1.user_id = f1.user_id1
    INNER JOIN Friendship f2 ON f1.user_id2 = f2.user_id1
    INNER JOIN User u2 ON f2.user_id2 = u2.user_id
    WHERE u1.user_id < u2.user_id
      AND EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM Friendship f3 
        WHERE f3.user_id1 = u1.user_id AND f3.user_id2 = u2.user_id
      )
    GROUP BY u1.user_id, u2.user_id
    HAVING COUNT(f1.user_id2) > 0;
    """
    mutual_results = conn.execute(mutual_query).fetchall()
    
    # Query 3: Friend-of-friend
    foaf_query = """
    SELECT u1.username AS user1, u3.username AS user2
    FROM Friendship f1
    JOIN Friendship f2 ON f1.user_id2 = f2.user_id1
    JOIN User u1 ON f1.user_id1 = u1.user_id
    JOIN User u3 ON f2.user_id2 = u3.user_id
    WHERE NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM Friendship f3 
        WHERE (f3.user_id1 = f1.user_id1 AND f3.user_id2 = f2.user_id2)
           OR (f3.user_id1 = f2.user_id2 AND f3.user_id2 = f1.user_id1)
    )
    GROUP BY u1.user_id, u3.user_id
    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT f1.user_id2) = 1;
    """
    foaf_results = conn.execute(foaf_query).fetchall()
    
    # Query 5: Recommendations
    recommend_query = """
    SELECT u1.username, u2.username
    FROM User u1
    JOIN User u2 ON u1.city = u2.city AND u1.user_id < u2.user_id
    WHERE NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM Friendship f 
        WHERE (f.user_id1 = u1.user_id AND f.user_id2 = u2.user_id)
           OR (f.user_id1 = u2.user_id AND f.user_id2 = u1.user_id)
    );
    """
    recommend_results = conn.execute(recommend_query).fetchall()
    
    # Combine results into FriendConnection objects
    results = []
    for res in mutual_results + foaf_results + recommend_results:
        results.append(FriendConnection(user1_name=res[0], user2_name=res[1]))
    
    return results

    );

    recommend_results = conn.execute(recommend_query).fetchall()
    
    # Compile results into FriendConnection objects
    results = []
    for res in mutual_results + foaf_results + recommend_results:
        results.append(FriendConnection(res[0], res[1]))
    
    return results
```</answer>

#include <ranges>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sstream>
#include <stdexcept>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

#include "calculate_order_metrics.hpp"

namespace calculate_order_metrics {

struct OrderItem {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> item_id;
  int order_id;
  int product_id;
  double unit_price;
  int quantity;
  std::optional<double> discount_rate;
  std::optional<double> tax_rate;
};

struct OrderMetrics {
  int item_id;
  int order_id;
  int product_id;
  double unit_price;
  int quantity;
  double discount_rate;
  double tax_rate;
  double gross_total;
  double discounted_total;
  double final_total;
  double unit_cost;
  int quantity_group;
  double estimated_profit;
};

std::vector<OrderMetrics> calculate_order_metrics_impl(
    const std::vector<OrderItem>& _items) {
  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

    // Create the OrderItem table.
    const auto create_table_stmt =
        sqlgen::to_sql(sqlgen::create_table_as(
            "OrderItem", sqlgen::make_table<OrderItem>(), "OR REPLACE"));
    conn->execute(_items.begin(), _items.end(), [&](const auto&) {
      return create_table_stmt;
    });

    // Insert all items into the table.
    const auto insert_stmt = sqlgen::to_sql(sqlgen::insert("OrderItem"));
    for (const auto& item : _items) {
      conn->execute(insert_stmt, sqlgen::to_named_tuple(item));
    }

    // Execute the query to calculate metrics.
    const auto query = R"(
SELECT 
  item_id,
  order_id,
  product_id,
  unit_price,
  quantity,
  COALESCE(discount_rate, 0.0) as discount_rate,
  COALESCE(tax_rate, 0.0) as tax_rate,
  (unit_price * quantity) as gross_total,
  (unit_price * quantity * (1 - COALESCE(discount_rate, 0.0)/100)) as discounted_total,
  (unit_price * quantity * (1 - COALESCE(discount_rate, 0.0)/100) * (1 + COALESCE(tax_rate, 0.0)/100)) as final_total,
  (unit_price * quantity / quantity) as unit_cost,
  (quantity % 10) as quantity_group,
  (unit_price * 0.3) as estimated_profit
FROM OrderItem;
)";

    auto res = conn->execute(_items.begin(), _items.end(), [&](const auto&) {
      return query;
    });

    // Read the results into OrderMetrics.
    std::vector<OrderMetrics> metrics;
    for (auto& row : res) {
      OrderMetrics om;
      om.item_id = row.get<int>("item_id");
      om.order_id = row.get<int>("order_id");
      om.product_id = row.get<int>("product_id");
      om.unit_price = row.get<double>("unit_price");
      om.quantity = row.get<int>("quantity");
      om.discount_rate = row.get<double>("discount_rate");
      om.tax_rate = row.get<double>("tax_rate");
      om.gross_total = row.get<double>("gross_total");
      om.discounted_total = row.get<double>("discounted_total");
      om.final_total = row.get<double>("final_total");
      om.unit_cost = row.get<double>("unit_cost");
      om.quantity_group = row.get<int>("quantity_group");
      om.estimated_profit = row.get<double>("estimated_profit");
      metrics.push_back(std::move(om));
    }

    return metrics;
  } catch (std::exception& e) {
    std::cerr << "Error: " << e.what() << std::endl;
    return {};
  }
}  // namespace calculate_order_metrics

}  // namespace calculate_order_metrics

<answer>```cpp
#include <optional>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sstream>
#include <stdexcept>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

#include "sqlgen/internal/collect/vector.hpp"
#include "sqlgen/internal/strings/strings.hpp"
#include "sqlgen/sqlite/connect.hpp"

namespace read_single_record_by_id {

struct Customer {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> customer_id;
  std::string name;
  std::string email;
};

Customer read_single_record_by_id(int id) {
  const auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  const auto query = "SELECT * FROM Customer WHERE customer_id = ?";
  const auto params = std::vector<int>({id});
  const auto customers = conn->template read<Customer>(query, params);

  if (customers.size() != 1) {
    throw std::runtime_error("Expected exactly one customer, but got " +
                             std::to_string(customers.size()));
  }

  return customers[0];
}

}  // namespace read_single_record_by_id
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace join_orders_customers_payments {

struct Customer {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> customer_id;
  std::string name;
};

struct Order {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> order_id;
  int customer_id;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> order_date;
};

struct Payment {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> payment_id;
  int order_id;
  double amount;
};

struct Consolidated {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> order_id;
  std::string customer_name;
  double amount;
};

std::vector<Consolidated> join_orders_customers_payments() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::make_sqlite_conn("test_db.sqlite");
  auto session = sqlgen::Session<sqlgen::SQLiteConn>::make(conn);

  const auto query = session->from<Customer>()
      .join<Order>(ON: Customer.customer_id == Order.customer_id)
      .join<Payment>(ON: Order.order_id == Payment.order_id)
      .select(Order.order_id, Customer.name.as("customer_name"), Payment.amount)
      .order_by(Payment.amount.desc())
      .to_sql();

  const auto results = session->execute<Consolidated>(query);

  return results;
}
}  // namespace join_orders_customers_payments
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>
```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace column_is_null_checks {

struct Profile {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> profile_id;
  std::string display_name;
  std::optional<std::string> bio;
  std::optional<std::string> avatar_url;
};

struct Result {
  int profile_id;
  std::string display_name;
};

std::vector<Result> column_is_null_checks() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  auto session = conn->begin_transaction();

  const auto query = session->selectFrom<Profile>(Profile::profile_id, Profile::display_name)
      .where(Profile::bio.is_null() || Profile::avatar_url.is_null());

  std::vector<Result> results;
  for (const auto& row : query) {
    Result res;
    res.profile_id = row.profile_id;
    res.display_name = std::move(row.display_name);
    results.push_back(std::move(res));
  }

  return results;
}

}  // namespace column_is_null_checks

```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>
#include <optional>

namespace full_join_suppliers_products {

struct Supplier {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> supplier_id;
  std::string name;
};

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  int supplier_id;
  std::string name;
};

struct SupProd {
  std::string supplier_name;
  std::optional<std::string> product_name;
};

std::vector<SupProd> full_join_suppliers_products() {
  auto session = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  auto query = session.selectFrom<Supplier, Product>(
      sqlgen::JoinType::full_join,
      "s", "p", "s.supplier_id = p.supplier_id")
      .fields(
          sqlgen::coalesce("s.name", "").as("supplier_name"),
          "p.name".as("product_name")
      )
      .to<std::vector<SupProd>>());
  return query.value();
}

}  // namespace full_join_suppliers_products
```
</answer>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>
#include <optional>
#include <string>

namespace analyze_department_employees {

struct JoinResult {
  std::optional<std::string> department_name;
  std::optional<std::string> first_name;
  std::optional<std::string> last_name;
  std::optional<double> salary;
};

struct JoinAnalysis {
  std::vector<JoinResult> inner_join_results;
  std::vector<JoinResult> left_join_results;
  std::vector<JoinResult> right_join_results;
  std::vector<JoinResult> full_join_results;
};

JoinAnalysis analyze_department_employees() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

  // INNER JOIN
  auto inner_sql = R"(
    SELECT d.department_name, e.first_name, e.last_name, e.salary
    FROM Department d
    INNER JOIN Employee e ON d.department_id = e.department_id;
  )";
  auto inner_res = conn->execute(inner_sql);
  for (auto& row : inner_res) {
    JoinResult res;
    res.department_name = row["department_name"].get<std::string>();
    res.first_name = row["first_name"].get<std::string>();
    res.last_name = row["last_name"].get<std::string>();
    res.salary = row["salary"].get<double>();
    JoinAnalysis::inner_join_results.emplace_back(std::move(res));
  }

  // LEFT JOIN
  auto left_sql = R"(
    SELECT d.department_name, e.first_name, e.last_name, e.salary
    FROM Employee e
    LEFT JOIN Department d ON e.department_id = d.department_id;
  )";
  auto left_res = conn->execute(left_sql);
  for (auto& row : left_res) {
    JoinResult res;
    res.department_name = row["department_name"].get<std::string>();
    res.first_name = row["first_name"].get<std::string>();
    res.last_name = row["last_name"].get<std::string>();
    res.salary = row["salary"].get<double>();
    JoinAnalysis::left_join_results.emplace_back(std::move(res));
  }

  // RIGHT JOIN
  auto right_sql = R"(
    SELECT d.department_name, e.first_name, e.last_name, e.salary
    FROM Employee e
    RIGHT JOIN Department d ON e.department_id = d.department_id;
  )";
  auto right_res = conn->execute(right_sql);
  for (auto& row : right_res) {
    JoinResult res;
    res.department_name = row["department_name"].get<std::string>();
    res.first_name = row["first_name"].get<std::string>();
    res.last_name = row["last_name"].get<std::string>();
    res.salary = row["salary"].get<double>();
    JoinAnalysis::right_join_results.emplace_back(std::move(res));
  }

  // FULL JOIN (simulated with UNION)
  auto full_sql = R"(
    SELECT d.department_name, e.first_name, e.last_name, e.salary
    FROM Employee e
    LEFT JOIN Department d ON e.department_id = d.department_id
    UNION
    SELECT d.department_name, e.first_name, e.last_name, e.salary
    FROM Employee e
    RIGHT JOIN Department d ON e.department_id = d.department_id;
  )";
  auto full_res = conn->execute(full_sql);
  for (auto& row : full_res) {
    JoinResult res;
    res.department_name = row["department_name"].get<std::string>();
    res.first_name = row["first_name"].get<std::string>();
    res.last_name = row["last_name"].get<std::string>();
    res.salary = row["salary"].get<double>();
    JoinAnalysis::full_join_results.emplace_back(std::move(res));
  }

  return JoinAnalysis{};
}

}  // namespace analyze_department_employees

<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <string>

namespace delete_with_complex_condition {

struct User {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  int age;
  std::string status;
};

inline std::string delete_with_complex_condition() {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;
  const auto query =
      sqlgen::DeleteFrom<User, Where<(User_.age() < 18) || (User_.status() == "inactive")>>{};
  return query.to_sql();
}
}  // namespace delete_with_complex_condition
```</answer>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>
#include <optional>

namespace coalesce_function {

struct Profile {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  std::optional<std::string> nickname;
};

struct NicknameResult {
  std::string display_name;
};

inline auto coalesce_function() {
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection(":memory:");
  auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Profile, NicknameResult>(
      sqlgen::coalesce(col("nickname"), "Anonymous").as("display_name"));
  return conn.execute(query).transform([](auto&& res) {
    return std::move(res);
  });
}

}  // namespace coalesce_function
</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <ranges>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sstream>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

#include "get_top_products.hpp"

namespace get_top_products {

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  std::string product_name;
  std::string category;
  double price;
};

struct Sales {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> sale_id;
  int product_id;
  int quantity;
  sqlgen::Date sale_date;
};

struct TopProduct {
  int product_id;
  std::string product_name;
  std::string category;
  double total_sales;
};

std::vector<TopProduct> get_top_products(size_t limit_count) {
  using namespace std::ranges::views;

  const auto db_path = "test_db.sqlite";

  try {
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::Connection::make(db_path);
    if (!conn) {
      return {};
    }

    const auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Product>()
        .fields(
            Product::product_id,
            Product::product_name,
            Product::category,
            sqlgen::sum(Product::price * Sales::quantity).as("total_sales")
        )
        .join<Sales>(sqlgen::JoinType::inner_join, Sales::product_id == Product::product_id)
        .where(Sales::sale_date != "0000-00-00")  // Prevent invalid dates
        .group_by(
            Product::product_id,
            Product::product_name,
            Product::category
        )
        .order_by(sqlgen::col("total_sales").desc())
        .limit(limit_count);

    const auto res = conn->execute(query);

    std::vector<TopProduct> result;
    for (const auto& row : res) {
      TopProduct tp;
      tp.product_id = row.get<int>("product_id");
      tp.product_name = row.get<std::string>("product_name");
      tp.category = row.get<std::string>("category");
      tp.total_sales = row.get<double>("total_sales");
      result.emplace_back(std::move(tp));
    }

    return result;
  } catch (std::exception& e) {
    std::cerr << "Error: " << e.what() << std::endl;
    return {};
  }
}

}  // namespace get_top_products
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <unordered_map>

namespace update_inventory_stock {

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  int stock;
};

bool update_inventory_stock(const std::unordered_map<int, int>& received) {
  using namespace sqlgen;
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;
  auto conn_res = sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  if (!conn_res) return false;

  auto txn_res = begin_transaction(conn_res.value());
  if (!txn_res) return false;
  auto txn = txn_res.value();
  
  for (const auto& [pid, qty] : received) {
    auto query = "UPDATE Product SET stock = stock + ? WHERE product_id = ?";
    auto exec_res = execute(txn, query, qty, pid);
    if (!exec_res) {
      return false;
    }
  }
  
  auto commit_res = commit(txn);
  return commit_res.has_value();
}

}  // namespace update_inventory_stock
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <ranges>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sstream>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

#include "create_view_order_summary.hpp"

namespace create_view_order_summary {

struct Order {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> order_id;
  std::string customer;
};

struct OrderItem {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> item_id;
  int order_id;
  double unit_price;
  int quantity;
};

struct OrderSummary {
  static constexpr const char* tablename = "OrderSummaryView";
  static constexpr bool is_view = true;
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> order_id;
  std::string customer;
  double total;
};

std::vector<OrderSummary> create_view_order_summary() {
  const auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");

  const std::string create_view_sql =
      "CREATE VIEW OrderSummaryView AS "
      "SELECT o.order_id, o.customer, SUM(oi.unit_price * oi.quantity) AS total "
      "FROM Order o "
      "INNER JOIN OrderItem oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id "
      "GROUP BY o.order_id, o.customer;";

  conn->execute(create_view_sql);

  const auto query_result = conn->template select_from<OrderSummary>().all();
  return query_result;
}

}  // namespace create_view_order_summary
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <ranges>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <sstream>
#include <stdexcept>
#include <type_traits>

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

#include "weekday_sales_analysis.hpp"

namespace weekday_sales_analysis {

struct Sales {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> sale_id;
  int product_id;
  int quantity;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> sale_time;
};

struct WeekdayStats {
  int weekday;
  int total_quantity;
};

std::vector<WeekdayStats> weekday_sales_analysis() {
  const auto extract_weekday = [](const auto& _sales) -> int {
    return _sales.sale_time.value().day_of_week();
  };

  const auto to_weekday_stats = [](const auto& _group) -> WeekdayStats {
    return {static_cast<int>(_group.key()), _group.values().transform([](const auto& _v) { return _v; }).value_or(0)};
  };

  return sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite")
      .transform([](auto&& _conn) {
        using Namespaces = sqlgen::internal::Namespaces;
        const auto query =
            _conn.template prepare_statement<Namespaces::std::vector<Namespaces::rfl::Tuple<int, int>>>(
                _conn.select_from<Sales>(Namespaces::rfl::named_tuple_t<Sales>(),
                                           Namespaces::rfl::field_names_t<Sales>()).transform([](auto&& _it) {
                                          return _it
                                              .select(extract_weekday, "total_quantity"_c)
                                              .where([](const auto&) { return true; })
                                              .group_by("weekday"_c)
                                              .order_by("weekday"_c)
                                              .to<Namespaces::std::vector<Namespaces::rfl::Tuple<int, int>>>();
                                        }));
        return _conn.execute(_conn.to_sql(query)).transform([&](const auto&) {
          return _conn.read<Namespaces::std::vector<Namespaces::rfl::Tuple<int, int>>>(query)
              .transform([](auto&& _vec) {
                return Namespaces::rfl::apply(
                    [](auto... _ptrs) {
                      return std::vector<WeekdayStats>({WeekdayStats{static_cast<int>(_ptrs.first), _ptrs.second}...});
                    },
                    _vec);
              });
        });
      })
      .value_or(std::vector<WeekdayStats>());
}

}  // namespace weekday_sales_analysis
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <string>

namespace update_with_arithmetic {

struct Player {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> id;
  int score;
};

std::string update_with_arithmetic() {
  auto score_col = column_t<Player, &Player::score>{};
  auto query = sqlgen::update<Player>().set(score_col, score_col + 10);
  return query.to_sql();
}

}  // namespace update_with_arithmetic

2. Write a SQL query that groups posts by user, counts them, and averages the content length.
3. Execute the query and map the results into the Stats struct.
4. Return the vector of Stats.

Let me think about the SQL query. The query should select from the Post table, join with the User table on user_id, group by user_id and name, count the posts, and calculate the average length of content.

So since we're grouping by user, we need to include user_id and name from the User table. So the correct approach is to join User and Post on user_id, then group by user_id and name.

So the SQL would be something like:

SELECT u.user_id, u.name, COUNT(p.post_id) AS post_count, AVG(LENGTH(p.content)) AS avg_length

GROUP BY u.user_id, u.name;

In sqlgen, you can use the select_from function. Let's see. The code would involve creating a connection, then building the query.
